meta.title: Top Agent Frameworks Q2 2026 — Two Views
meta.subtitle: The top 10 autonomous agent frameworks ranked by OpenAI and Gemini — compared side by side
meta.author: Imbila.AI

summary: Two major AI labs — OpenAI (via o3) and Google (via Gemini Deep Research) — each independently produced a top-10 ranking of autonomous agent frameworks on 26 March 2026. They identified 14 unique frameworks total, with 6 appearing on both lists. LangGraph and OpenClaw rank #1 and #2 on both. The differences reflect methodological choices: OpenAI focused on production readiness and primary GitHub sources; Gemini cast a wider net including low-code platforms, TypeScript-first frameworks, and the Ollama runtime evolution.

products.name: LangChain + LangGraph
products.description: Broadest agent engineering ecosystem with durable graphs, HITL, and vast integrations. Enterprise standard with directed cyclic graphs, checkpointing, and 34.5M monthly downloads. Klarna's LangGraph bot reportedly saves $60M handling two-thirds of inbound inquiries.

products.name: OpenClaw
products.description: On-device execution pioneer with multi-channel support (WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, Discord), skills/plugins, daemon runtime, ReAct loop, heartbeat scheduling, and 100+ built-in capabilities. Originally named 'Clawd'. Grew to 337k GitHub stars faster than any prior project.

products.name: LlamaIndex
products.description: Strongest agentic data and workflows stack with massive integration catalogue. 48k GitHub stars. On OpenAI top 10 at #3; Gemini honourable mention.

products.name: CrewAI
products.description: Opinionated multi-agent 'teams + flows' with memory and guardrails in core. Sociological abstraction using role-playing crews. Raised $18M Series A. 47.3k GitHub stars.

products.name: Semantic Kernel
products.description: Model-agnostic enterprise SDK with mature plugin model supporting code, prompts, and MCP. 27.6k GitHub stars. MIT licence. OpenAI list only (#5, score 80).

products.name: AutoGen
products.description: Proven multi-agent conversation patterns. Microsoft positions Agent Framework as successor. Mixed licence. 56.3k GitHub stars. OpenAI list only (#6, score 78).

products.name: Haystack
products.description: Production pipelines and agent workflows with explicit RAG control and routing. Apache-2.0 licence. 24.6k GitHub stars. OpenAI list only (#7, score 77).

products.name: AgentScope
products.description: Developer-centric agents with companion sandbox runtime for deployment. Apache-2.0 licence. 20.3k GitHub stars. OpenAI list only (#8, score 76).

products.name: Microsoft Agent Framework
products.description: AutoGen + Semantic Kernel successor with graph workflows, multi-provider support. Still RC. MIT licence. 8.2k GitHub stars. On OpenAI list (#9, score 74); Gemini honourable mention.

products.name: smolagents
products.description: Lightweight 'agents that think in code' with strong sandboxing and model-agnostic design. Agents write Python directly, bypassing JSON serialisation for raw efficiency. Apache-2.0 licence. 26.3k GitHub stars.

products.name: Ollama
products.description: Evolved from inference engine to agent runtime with native tool execution in v0.14, including approval UI and deny-lists. Gemini list only (#3). OpenAI explicitly excluded it as a runtime rather than a framework.

products.name: Dify
products.description: Visual drag-and-drop Backend-as-a-Service for AI agents. 1.4M machines deployed across 175 countries. $30M funding. 129.8k GitHub stars. Gemini list only (#5).

products.name: OpenAI Agents SDK
products.description: Handoffs, guardrails, and tracing primitives. 10.3M monthly downloads. Provider-agnostic. 19k GitHub stars. Gemini list only (#6).

products.name: Pydantic AI
products.description: Strict output schema enforcement with auto-retry on validation failure. Not a full orchestrator — a validation layer to pair with LangGraph or similar. Gemini list only (#8).

products.name: Mastra
products.description: Full-stack TypeScript agents for Next.js — the missing layer between Vercel AI SDK and production. The only TS-first option with production-ready agent primitives for edge deployment. Gemini list only (#9).

products.name: n8n
products.description: Pivoted from Zapier-like automation to AI-native multi-agent orchestrator with 422 integrations. 160k GitHub stars. Gemini list only (#10).

use_when: Enterprise state machines: LangGraph. Both reports rank it #1. Durable execution, checkpointing, HITL, Fortune 500 credibility.
Multi-agent teams: CrewAI. Ranked #4 on both lists. Role-based 'crew' abstraction is the fastest way to prototype collaborative agents.
Code-first lightweight agents: smolagents. On both lists. Write and execute code in sandboxed environments with minimal overhead.
On-device / local-first: OpenClaw. Both rank it #2. Broad system access demands disciplined security hardening.
TypeScript shop: Mastra (Gemini only). The only TS-first option with production-ready agent primitives for Next.js/edge.
Non-technical team: Dify or n8n (Gemini only). Visual, low-code builders that let product managers wire up agent workflows.
Type safety obsessed: Pydantic AI (Gemini only). A validation layer you pair with LangGraph or similar.
Microsoft enterprise stack: Semantic Kernel or MS Agent Framework (OpenAI list). Accept the RC risk on Agent Framework, or use the stable Semantic Kernel SDK.
Privacy-first, fully offline: Ollama (Gemini only). Legitimately agentic now with v0.14 native agent loops.
skip_when: Framework choice is inseparable from your guardrails strategy, execution isolation, and state durability approach. The 'demo tuxedo' is real — what works in a controlled environment will fail differently in production.
Budget at least as much time for security, observability, and integration plumbing as you do for the agent logic itself.
OpenClaw's broad system access demands disciplined security hardening — the blast radius of a misconfigured full-stack agent is severe.
Ollama has legitimate agentic capabilities in v0.14 but limited orchestration abstractions compared to full frameworks.

use_cases.title: Enterprise Deployments
use_cases.description: LangGraph's durable state machines paired with Pydantic AI's validation layer. The graph handles orchestration, checkpointing, and human-in-the-loop approvals. Pydantic catches malformed outputs before they touch a production database. Haystack enters the mix when RAG pipelines need explicit, auditable retrieval control.

use_cases.title: Prototyping and Proof-of-Concepts
use_cases.description: CrewAI's role-based abstraction can get multi-agent demos running in hours rather than weeks. When agents need to write and run code (data transformation, analysis scripts), smolagents with E2B sandboxing is a natural complement.

use_cases.title: Learning Path: Visual to Multi-Agent to Production
use_cases.description: Start with n8n or Dify for visual intuition, graduate to CrewAI for multi-agent concepts, then LangGraph for production patterns.

timeline.title: The LLM Wrapper Era
timeline.detail: LangChain and LlamaIndex launch as thin abstraction layers over GPT-3/4 APIs. Agents are simple ReAct loops — impressive demos, fragile in production. AutoGen introduces multi-agent conversation patterns.

timeline.title: Frameworks Get Serious
timeline.detail: CrewAI raises $18M Series A. LangGraph emerges as the state-machine layer. Smolagents launches with 'agents that think in code.' Haystack ships v2 with pipeline-first architecture.

timeline.title: LangChain + LangGraph Hit 1.0 GA
timeline.detail: Both projects commit to API stability. Durable execution, checkpointing, and HITL become first-class primitives. Klarna's LangGraph bot reportedly saves $60M handling two-thirds of inbound inquiries.

timeline.title: OpenClaw Is Born
timeline.detail: Originally 'Clawd' — launches as a full-stack personal agent running on your own devices. Multi-channel (WhatsApp, Telegram, Slack, Discord). Grows to 337k GitHub stars faster than any prior project.

timeline.title: Convergence Begins
timeline.detail: Microsoft merges AutoGen + Semantic Kernel into the unified Agent Framework. Ollama ships v0.14 with native agent loops. Dify raises $30M and hits 130k stars. MCP becomes the interop standard everyone rallies around.

timeline.title: The Q2 Snapshot
timeline.detail: Both OpenAI and Gemini produce independent top-10 rankings on 26 March 2026. The ecosystem has 14+ serious contenders. The 'plumbing problem' — integration, observability, schema drift — is now the primary blocker, not model intelligence.

sources.title: OpenAI o3 report: Top open-source AI-native agent frameworks for autonomous agents in Q2 2026 (26 Mar 2026)
Gemini Deep Research report: The State of Agentic Execution (Q2 2026) (26 Mar 2026)
